name: UCSB

confRecord.note: Big West Reg Season Champions. Lost Tourn Final to UC Irvine 1-2. NCAA at-large bid.

notable: Manu Duah — #1 overall 2025 (highest ever Big West pick)

acuAlignNote: Strong — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, sport psych all covered. Motor development/control less structured than at UF or Indiana.

gpa.note: UC system is merit-aware. International student GPA review differs from domestic.

fin.internationalNote: UCSB out-of-state COA is ~$66k/yr (2025-26). UC nonresident tuition increase applies — same regents policy as UCLA. International students qualify for athletic aid. Athletic package is primary lever. Santa Barbara living costs moderate vs LA.

culture.vibe: Laid-back Californian surf culture. UCSB is famous for its relaxed vibe — students study hard but also surf, bike to class, and socialise on the beach. Isla Vista (adjacent neighbourhood) is one of the most social student areas in California.
culture.thingsToDo: Surfing at Goleta Beach (walking distance), biking along the coast, kayaking, Santa Barbara farmers markets, wine country day trips, hiking in the Santa Ynez Mountains, whale watching, State Street restaurants.
culture.socialScene: Very social campus. Large student population but feels intimate due to coastal setting. International students welcomed — strong surf and outdoor culture. Less city-oriented than LA but more relaxed.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★★ Closest climate and lifestyle to Sydney in entire guide. Beach at your doorstep. Relaxed atmosphere. Less urban bustle than LA but arguably better day-to-day liveability for an Australian.

facilityDetails.stadium: Harder Stadium — 17,000 capacity, one of the largest dedicated soccer stadiums in US college soccer. Natural grass surface, full broadcast infrastructure, video scoreboard, 360° permanent seating.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields adjacent to Harder Stadium. 1 additional satellite training field on east campus. All-weather surfaces, permanent floodlighting. Santa Barbara climate allows year-round outdoor training.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UCSB Athletics Weight Room — 15,000 sq ft shared facility with strength coaches for all Olympic sports. Free weights, Olympic platforms, Keiser pneumatic systems. Soccer-specific conditioning blocks scheduled with dedicated S&C coach.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science teaching labs with metabolic carts (VO2 max), body composition analysis (DEXA), and functional movement screening. GPS tracking via Statsports system on all training sessions. Video analysis system (Hudl) with iPad tablets for all players.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Dedicated athletic trainer for soccer. On-site treatment room with ultrasound, e-stim, ice baths. Goleta Valley Cottage Hospital sports medicine partnership for imaging and surgical consultation.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: UCSB Department of Kinesiology: teaching labs for biomechanics, motor learning, exercise physiology. Bren Hall science facilities available to kinesiology students. Marine Science Institute provides additional lab access. Pre-PT advising formally integrated into kinesiology program.
facilityDetails.extras: UCSB Recreation Center (one of the most highly rated campus rec facilities in the USA) — climbing wall, Olympic pool, fitness studios. Team video room in Harder Stadium. Beach volleyball courts, surfboard rental, ocean swimming all on campus.
facilityDetails.note: Harder Stadium's 17,000 capacity creates an elite match atmosphere unmatched in D1 outside the top 15 programs nationally. The campus recreation environment is extraordinary — ocean, mountains, and world-class facilities all accessible.

coach.name: Tim Vom Steeg
coach.title: Head Coach (27th season 2025)
coach.profile: 27th season at UCSB 2025. Career record 324-152-76. 2006 NCAA National Champion. National CoY 2004 and 2006. 4x Big West CoY. 14 Big West Championships (12 reg season + 2 tourn). 32 MLS draftees including Manu Duah #1 overall 2025. UCSB alum BA History 1989, MA 2000. His four sons all played for UCSB. 2025 Big West Regular Season Champions.

rec: Mediterranean climate identical to Sydney. Manu Duah #1 pick proves D1 pro calibre. Strong kinesiology. Beach campus lifestyle match.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr2 improves: 2 rising seniors clear after Yr1, opening spots. Medium risk. Olivier builds on proven Yr1.

staff.name: Greg Wilson
staff.bg: Long-tenured associate HC. Recruiting and tactical development.

staff.name: Matias Fernandez
staff.bg: GK development.

staff.name: Carson Vom Steeg
staff.bg: Joined May 2025. UCSB alum. Former US U-18 National Team captain. Stanford NCAA champion 2017. USL career: Loudoun United (DC United reserve) and Memphis 901 FC.

staff.name: Kian Kohan
staff.bg: Program operations.

name: Cal Poly
acuAlignNote: Strong kinesiology — learn-by-doing philosophy. Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics all covered with strong lab component.

fin.internationalNote: Cal Poly out-of-state COA is ~$32k/yr (2025-26). CSU nonresident tuition is lower than UC campuses. Athletic scholarship can cover a significant portion of costs. Academic merit aid is limited for international students under CSU policy. San Luis Obispo has lower living costs than LA or the Bay Area.

gpa.note: Cal Poly's 'learn by doing' model is unique. Athletic scholarship pathway available.

confRecord.note: Big West runner-up. Qualified for tournament.

coach.name: Steve Sampson
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Steve Sampson has built Cal Poly into a perennial Big West top-2 program with back-to-back regular season titles (2023, 2024). San Luis Obispo is consistently ranked the most liveable college town in California. Learn-by-doing kinesiology model means strong lab and practical experience alongside soccer development.

facilityDetails.stadium: Alex G. Spanos Stadium — multi-sport facility, Cal Poly soccer field. Natural grass.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Mustang athletic fields — dedicated soccer training. Year-round outdoor training in SLO's Mediterranean climate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Cal Poly Athletics S&C facility — fully equipped, soccer-specific programming.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department labs — exercise physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. Learn-by-doing means lab hours integrated into coursework.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Cal Poly Athletics sports medicine. French Hospital Medical Center (SLO) — team physician network for imaging and surgical consultation.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs: biomechanics motion capture, exercise physiology metabolic testing. Learn-by-doing curriculum includes practicum placements at SLO physical therapy and rehabilitation clinics.
facilityDetails.extras: San Luis Obispo — consistently ranked best college town in California. Wine country (Paso Robles 30 min), Pismo Beach (20 min), hiking Los Padres National Forest. Low cost of living. Bike-able campus. No gridlock traffic. Cal Poly Rec Center — Olympic pool, fitness facilities.
facilityDetails.note: Cal Poly's best asset is the combination of Big West-calibre soccer, low cost, and the SLO lifestyle. Not elite-facility level but well-supported and improving.

culture.vibe: Cal Poly SLO — the most beloved college town in California. Hands-on, practical, no-pretension culture. 'Learn by doing' is a genuine institutional identity, not a slogan. Agriculture, engineering, and kinesiology all share the same practical ethos.
culture.thingsToDo: Pismo Beach and Avila Beach (20 min), wine tasting Paso Robles, hiking Bishop Peak, Farmers Market (Thursday nights on Higuera Street), San Luis Obispo Mission, Morro Bay, Big Sur day trip. One of the best outdoor activity corridors in California.
culture.socialScene: Tight-knit campus with strong athletics culture. SLO is a college town — the city orbits the university. Soccer is respected. Great balance of outdoors and social life without LA sprawl.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ SLO is an exceptional lifestyle match — warm, coastal proximity, outdoor-focused, low cost. Soccer is genuinely competitive (Big West top-2). Best value non-UC option in California.
culture.lifestyleTags: SLO lifestyle, wine country, coastal proximity, low cost

staff.name: Steve Sampson
staff.bg: Head Coach. Back-to-back Big West regular season titles 2023-24. SLO campus is one of California's best athletic environments.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Best value California coastal option. SLO campus lifestyle is outstanding — beaches, wine country, mountains. Low cost for a California D1.

name: UC Davis
acuAlignNote: Exercise Biology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. UC Davis Medical Center provides strong clinical access for pre-PT.

fin.internationalNote: UC Davis out-of-state COA is ~$58k/yr (2025-26). UC nonresident tuition surcharge applies. Athletic scholarship can cover 30–50% of COA. Academic merit limited for international students under UC policy. Davis has moderate cost of living relative to Bay Area campuses.

gpa.note: Selective UC campus. International athletes qualify for athletic aid.

confRecord.note: Defending Big West Tourn champs. Lost SF to UCSB 2-3.

coach.name: TBD
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: UC Davis program won the 2024 Big West Tournament Championship. UC Davis Medical Center (one of the top research hospitals in California) is available for pre-PT clinical placements. Davis is a bike-friendly college town adjacent to Sacramento — warm, clean, and affordable relative to Bay Area UC campuses.

facilityDetails.stadium: Aggie Soccer Field — dedicated natural grass stadium on UC Davis campus. 1,500 capacity.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: UC Davis athletic training grounds adjacent to Aggie Soccer Field. Year-round outdoor training in warm Central Valley climate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UC Davis Athletics S&C facility — full suite shared with Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Biology Department — UC Davis is a Tier 1 research university. Sports science labs with VO2 testing, biomechanics equipment.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UC Davis Athletics sports medicine team. UC Davis Medical Center (Level I Trauma Centre, Sacramento) — academic medical centre for pre-PT clinical access. Strong DPT articulation pathway through UC Davis Health.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Biology labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor control. UC Davis is ranked top-15 globally in biological sciences — the academic infrastructure is genuinely elite for pre-PT pathway.
facilityDetails.extras: Davis is one of the most bike-friendly cities in the USA. Sacramento (30 min drive) — major city with Kings NBA, Sacramento Republic (USL), and major healthcare hub. Lake Tahoe day trip (2 hrs). Napa Valley wine country (1 hr).
facilityDetails.note: UC Davis' best asset is the combination of Tier 1 research-university academic infrastructure and UC Davis Medical Center clinical access — both well above what the soccer program's profile would suggest.

culture.vibe: UC Davis is California's most relaxed major research university — bike-centric, agricultural roots, warm inland climate. Strong international student community. Academia-first but athletes are respected. Soccer team has surprising tournament pedigree.
culture.thingsToDo: Sacramento Kings (NBA), Sacramento Republic FC (USL), Farmer's Market (Saturdays), cycling the Putah Creek Trail, Lake Tahoe (2 hrs), Napa Valley (1 hr), San Francisco day trip (1.5 hrs), UC Davis Arboretum.
culture.socialScene: Mid-size UC campus (39k) — smaller feel than UCLA or Berkeley. College town of Davis is self-contained and walkable. Active outdoor culture — cycling, running, rec sports.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ UC Davis offers UC academic quality and a genuine 2024 tournament championship program. Not coastal — but warm, affordable relative to other UCs, and Sacramento provides city access. Strong pre-PT pathway through UC Davis Health.
culture.lifestyleTags: Bike-friendly, Sacramento access, UC Davis Health, warm inland

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: UC Davis head coach. 2024 Big West Tournament Champions.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: UC quality academics. Davis is a bike-friendly college town near Sacramento. Warm inland California — not coastal but pleasant.

name: UC Irvine
acuAlignNote: Exercise science covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. UC Irvine Medical Center provides strong pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: UC Irvine out-of-state COA is ~$55k/yr (2025-26). UC nonresident tuition surcharge applies. Athletic scholarship can cover 30–50% of COA. Academic merit limited for international students under UC policy. Orange County location with moderate-to-high living costs.

gpa.note: Selective UC campus. Athletic scholarship pathway available for international athletes.

confRecord.note: 2025 Big West Tournament Champions — beat UCSB 2-1 in final.

coach.name: Head Coach
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: UC Irvine claimed the 2025 Big West Tournament Championship, beating UCSB 2-1 in the final. UCI Medical Center (nationally ranked academic medical centre) provides exceptional pre-PT clinical access. Orange County location — warm, coastal proximity, 5 miles from Huntington Beach.

facilityDetails.stadium: Peter Aldrich Field — dedicated soccer stadium on UCI campus. Natural grass, 1,500 capacity.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Anteater athletic complex training grounds. Year-round outdoor training in OC Mediterranean climate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UCI Athletics S&C facility — full suite shared with Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs. UCI is a top-15 public research university — science infrastructure is excellent.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UCI Athletics sports medicine team. UCI Medical Center (Level I Trauma, nationally ranked for orthopaedics) provides pre-PT/Chiro clinical access. UCI Health partnership for student-athlete medical care.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs: physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. UCI Health DPT articulation pathway available. CHOC (Children's Hospital of Orange County — top-10 USA) is 10 min from campus.
facilityDetails.extras: Orange County — Huntington Beach (5 miles), Newport Beach (10 min), Laguna Beach (15 min). Disneyland (3 miles). LA access via I-405. LAFC, LA Galaxy, LAFC training grounds all accessible. Year-round warm weather. Irvine is consistently rated the safest large city in the USA.
facilityDetails.note: UCI's combination of 2025 Big West championship, UCI Health clinical access, and Orange County location creates the best lifestyle-academic package in the Big West below UCSB.

culture.vibe: UC Irvine is a rapidly rising research university in one of the most desirable locations in America. Irvine is clean, planned, and safe — the most liveable suburban city in Southern California. Strong international student community (30%+ international). Soccer program is on the rise.
culture.thingsToDo: Huntington Beach (5 miles), Newport Beach, Laguna Beach, Disneyland (3 miles), UCI arts and culture precinct, LA Galaxy/LAFC games (45 min), Crystal Cove State Park hiking, Balboa Island.
culture.socialScene: Large UC campus (36k) with strong international student culture. Irvine is suburban — less nightlife than LA but safer and cleaner. The beach corridor is the social centre. Good balance of study and lifestyle.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ UCI is the best balance of competitive Big West soccer (2025 champion), UCI Health clinical access, and elite OC lifestyle. Warm, coastal, safe, and rising academically. Second only to UCSB for Olivier's warm-coastal preference.
culture.lifestyleTags: OC beaches, UCI Health clinical, Irvine safety, coastal warm

courses: BIO SCI 94 — Cell and Molecular Biology

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: 2025 Big West Tournament Champions — beat UCSB 2-1 in the final.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Orange County location — warm, near beach, LA day trips. Strong lifestyle for Olivier. Soccer is mid-tier Big West.

name: UC Riverside
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Developing program — pre-PT support available.

fin.internationalNote: UC Riverside out-of-state COA is ~$50k/yr (2025-26). UC nonresident tuition surcharge applies. Athletic scholarship can cover 30–50% of COA. Academic merit limited for international students under UC policy. Inland Riverside has lower living costs than coastal UC campuses.

gpa.note: Accessible UC campus. Strong scholarship packages for international athletes.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for Big West Tournament.

coach.name: Head Coach
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: UC Riverside is a developing Big West program. Consistently lower-half Big West — best minutes opportunity in the conference for an international recruit. Inland Southern California location — warm and dry. UC Riverside Medical Centre available for pre-PT clinical access. LA and Orange County accessible by car (60-90 min).

facilityDetails.stadium: UCR Soccer Stadium — dedicated facility on campus. Natural grass.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Athletic complex training fields adjacent to soccer stadium. Hot inland climate means training begins early season.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UC Riverside Athletics S&C facility — standard UC suite.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department labs. UCR is an R1 research university — science infrastructure improving rapidly.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UCR Athletics sports medicine team. UC Riverside Medical Centre (Moreno Valley, 15 min) — academic medical facility. LA and OC hospital networks accessible for clinical placements.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs: anatomy, exercise physiology, biomechanics. UCR is the most accessible UC campus for international athletes — scholarship packages are competitive.
facilityDetails.extras: Inland Empire — Palm Springs (45 min), beaches (Orange County 60 min, San Diego 90 min), Coachella Valley. LA accessible via I-10. Riverside Mission Inn (historic landmark). UCR Botanic Gardens.
facilityDetails.note: UC Riverside is the minutes-first option in the Big West. Lower soccer pedigree but real D1 platform, UC degree, and Southern California location. For an international recruit who needs to play, UCR offers the clearest pathway.

culture.vibe: UC Riverside is the most accessible and diverse UC campus — strong international and first-generation student community. Inland Empire location means hot summers and mild winters. Less prestigious than coastal UCs but same UC degree framework and R1 research infrastructure.
culture.thingsToDo: Palm Springs (45 min), Joshua Tree National Park (1 hr), beaches of Orange County (60 min), San Diego (90 min), LA (60 min via I-10). Riverside Mission Inn, UCR arts district, Coachella Valley concerts.
culture.socialScene: Diverse campus (26k). Less traditional college town — Riverside is a suburban city. Students often travel to LA, OC, or Palm Springs on weekends. Lower-key social scene than coastal campuses.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ UC Riverside is the fallback option — minutes are most accessible but soccer quality and lifestyle rank lowest in the Big West. If Olivier needs to play from Year 1 at a UC campus and cost is a factor, UCR is the answer.
culture.lifestyleTags: Inland SoCal, minutes opportunity, accessible UC, Palm Springs access

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: UCR head coach. Building program in competitive Big West field.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Inland Southern California — hot and dry. More minutes opportunity than stronger UC campuses. Weakest soccer in this batch.

name: UC San Diego
acuAlignNote: Exercise and Rehabilitative Sciences — one of the most PT-aligned degrees in the UC system. Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, clinical rehabilitation all covered. UCSD Health for clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: UCSD out-of-state COA is ~$55k/yr (2025-26). UC nonresident tuition surcharge applies. Athletic scholarship can cover 30–50% of COA. Academic merit limited for international students under UC policy. La Jolla / San Diego location — high living costs.

gpa.note: Highly selective UC campus (recently elevated to major research university). Athletic scholarship available.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for Big West Tournament.
First Big West season (promoted to D1).

coach.name: Head Coach
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: UCSD is a rapidly elevating program — recently elevated to full Division I research university status. The Exercise and Rehabilitative Sciences degree is among the most PT-aligned in the UC system. UCSD Health (top-15 nationally for research) provides exceptional pre-PT/Chiro clinical access. La Jolla is one of the most beautiful campus settings in US college soccer.

facilityDetails.stadium: RIMAC Arena/Field complex — multi-use athletic facility. Soccer field adjacent to RIMAC Arena on main La Jolla campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: UCSD athletic fields at the Canyon Vista complex. Year-round outdoor training in San Diego's perfect climate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UCSD Athletics S&C — renovated facility at RIMAC Arena. Full suite for Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise and Rehabilitative Sciences Department — genuinely elite. UCSD is a top-7 public research university with medical school on campus. Research opportunities for student-athletes.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UCSD Athletics sports medicine team. UCSD Health (ranked top-15 nationally, Sulpizio Cardiovascular and Shiley Eye Institute) — one of the best medical centres in the USA for pre-PT/Chiro placements. Rady Children's Hospital (top-10 children's hospital USA) adjacent to campus.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise and Rehabilitative Sciences labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, clinical rehabilitation. UCSD School of Medicine and UCSD Health create the best pre-DPT academic environment in the Big West. Multiple DPT programs in San Diego — Touro (UCSD partnership), USD, and SDSU all accessible.
facilityDetails.extras: La Jolla — most beautiful UC campus setting. Pacific Ocean 5 min from campus. Black's Beach (clothing-optional, beneath campus cliff). La Jolla Cove snorkelling. Torrey Pines Golf (US Open venue). San Diego Zoo (20 min). Balboa Park museums. Tijuana border (30 min — weekend trip). San Diego Padres MLB (NL), San Diego FC (MLS 2025 expansion).
facilityDetails.note: UCSD is the guide's best academic-clinical environment for pre-DPT pathway — UCSD Health is world-class. La Jolla lifestyle is unmatched. The trade-off is a GPA requirement above Olivier's current level and a still-developing soccer program.

culture.vibe: UCSD is the most academic UC campus after Berkeley and UCLA — strong research identity, Nobel Prize history, Scripps Institution of Oceanography, Salk Institute all on campus. La Jolla is affluent and beautiful — Pacific Ocean views from the library. Soccer program is building under new Division I infrastructure.
culture.thingsToDo: La Jolla Cove, Torrey Pines State Reserve, Pacific Beach boardwalk, San Diego Padres (MLB), San Diego FC (MLS), San Diego Zoo, Balboa Park, Tijuana day trip (30 min), Coronado Island, Julian apple picking, wine country Temecula (1 hr).
culture.socialScene: Academic-first campus with strong research culture. La Jolla is a wealthy beach town — upscale social scene. Pacific Beach (5 min) is the party corridor. San Diego is a genuinely great city with excellent food, weather, and lifestyle. Strong international student community.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ UCSD is the best academic/clinical environment in the Big West but GPA is a barrier at 3.0+ minimum. If Olivier achieves the academic threshold, the combination of UCSD Health pre-DPT access + La Jolla lifestyle is exceptional. Soccer is still building.
culture.lifestyleTags: La Jolla prestige, UCSD Health clinical, San Diego lifestyle, pre-DPT pathway

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: UCSD head coach. Building program under new full D1 infrastructure at La Jolla.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: San Diego/La Jolla — best beach city in the guide after UCSB. Exercise and Rehabilitative Sciences is ideal for Olivier's DPT path. Rising program.

name: Long Beach State
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Long Beach Memorial Medical Center for pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: Long Beach State out-of-state COA is ~$30k/yr (2025-26). CSU nonresident tuition is lower than UC campuses. Athletic scholarship can cover a significant portion of costs. LA area living costs are high — factor into net cost. Academic merit limited for international students under CSU policy.

gpa.note: Very accessible CSU campus. Strong scholarship packages for international athletes.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for Big West Tournament.

coach.name: Head Coach
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Long Beach State Big West program in the heart of the LA metro. Beach campus — 2 miles from the Pacific Ocean. Long Beach Memorial Medical Center (nearby) provides pre-PT clinical access. LAFC, LA Galaxy, and LA's full MLS/USL ecosystem accessible for professional networking. Lowest cost full-profile D1 option in Southern California.

facilityDetails.stadium: CSULB Soccer Complex — on-campus natural grass field. Dedicated soccer facility.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Athletic complex training fields. Year-round outdoor training in LA's warm coastal climate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: CSULB Athletics S&C facility — functional shared suite.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department labs. CSU system — practical focus rather than research-heavy. Exercise physiology and biomechanics labs for coursework.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: CSULB Athletics sports medicine. Long Beach Memorial Medical Center (major hospital, 5 min drive) — clinical placement access for kinesiology students. Miller Children's and Women's Hospital Long Beach (adjacent to Memorial) for paediatric PT access.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. Strong practical curriculum. Pre-PT pathway through Long Beach Memorial and CHOC (Children's Hospital of OC, 20 min).
facilityDetails.extras: 2 miles from Long Beach waterfront and beaches. Queen Mary (iconic cruise ship hotel, 10 min). Aquarium of the Pacific. LA Galaxy (StubHub Center, 20 min), LAFC (BMO Stadium, 30 min). Long Beach Grand Prix (annual F1-style street race). Downtown Long Beach restaurant and bar scene.
facilityDetails.note: Long Beach State's best asset is location + value — LA lifestyle at CSU pricing. For Olivier, the combination of warm climate, beach access, LA pro soccer ecosystem, and low cost makes it one of the best value options in the guide.

culture.vibe: CSULB is a diverse, urban commuter-friendly campus with a growing residential culture. Long Beach is one of the most culturally diverse cities in California — food, music, and arts scene is excellent. Beach is 2 miles away. Soccer culture is building. Strong Latino and international student community.
culture.thingsToDo: Long Beach beaches, Belmont Shore restaurants, Shoreline Village, Queen Mary, Aquarium of the Pacific, LA Galaxy/LAFC games (20-30 min), Disneyland (30 min), downtown LA (30 min), Santa Monica/Venice (45 min), Hollywood (35 min).
culture.socialScene: Large commuter campus but growing residential population. Downtown Long Beach is an underrated social scene — Pine Avenue, 4th Street Retro Row, Bixby Knolls. Beach corridor on weekends. Great food from LA's diverse culinary culture.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Long Beach State is the best value lifestyle option in the guide for Olivier — warm, beach-adjacent, LA pro soccer access, very low cost, and accessible GPA. Soccer is lower-tier Big West but the city environment and professional networking offset this.
culture.lifestyleTags: LA beach lifestyle, lowest D1 cost, LAFC/Galaxy network, warm coastal

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: CSULB head coach. Building Big West program in LA metro — best value D1 environment in Southern California.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: LA metro + beach + very low cost = best value lifestyle option in the Big West. Soccer is mid-tier but LA career access is a real bonus.

name: Hawaii
acuAlignNote: Exercise and sports science — covers core subjects. Hawaii is geographically isolated, which limits some clinical placement options.

fin.internationalNote: Hawaii out-of-state COA is ~$42k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Honolulu cost of living is high relative to the mainland — factor into net cost. Merit aid available. Strong Pacific Rim and international student community.

gpa.note: Accessible state university. Travel costs to/from Australia are high — factored separately.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for Big West Tournament.

coach.name: Head Coach
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Hawaii is a unique program — island geography creates a close-knit team culture and a lifestyle unmatched anywhere in US college soccer. Consistently lower Big West but real minutes opportunity for international recruits. Strong Pacific Rim international student community. The Queen's Medical Center and Straub Medical Center provide pre-PT clinical access in Honolulu.

facilityDetails.stadium: Waipio Peninsula Soccer Stadium — dedicated natural grass stadium on Oahu. 3,500 capacity. 20 min from campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Lower Campus athletic fields. Year-round tropical outdoor training — warm but humid.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UH Athletics S&C facility at the Stan Sheriff Center complex. Shared with other Olympic sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise and Sports Science Department labs — functional but not research-tier. Geographic isolation limits some external research partnerships.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UH Athletics sports medicine team. Queen's Medical Center (Honolulu's premier hospital) — clinical placement access for pre-PT. Straub Medical Center — sports orthopaedics. Note: geographic isolation means fewer hospital networks than mainland programs.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: ESS labs: anatomy, physiology, exercise testing. Strong Pacific Rim health science community. Honolulu has a good physical therapy and rehabilitation market but fewer DPT programs than California.
facilityDetails.extras: Living on Oahu — Waikiki (20 min), North Shore surfing (45 min), Diamond Head hiking, Hanauma Bay snorkelling, Pearl Harbor, Polynesian Cultural Centre. Strong Pacific connection — closer to Australia than to mainland USA by time zone. International travel costs are significant.
facilityDetails.note: Hawaii's facilities are functional but not standout. The lifestyle advantage is unique — no other program in the guide offers tropical island living. Key trade-offs: geographic isolation, extra travel cost to Australia, and lower academic/soccer tier.

culture.vibe: Hawaii is the most unique cultural environment in US college sports — Aloha spirit, Pacific Islander culture, diverse international student community. Team culture is close-knit due to geographic isolation. Soccer players from Australia, New Zealand, and Asia find Hawaii a natural cultural bridge.
culture.thingsToDo: Waikiki, North Shore (Banzai Pipeline surfing), Diamond Head hike, Hanauma Bay, Pearl Harbor, Polynesian Cultural Centre, Lanikai Beach, Sunrise on Haleakala (Maui day trip), luaus, paddleboarding, outrigger canoe.
culture.socialScene: Small flagship campus (18k). Honolulu social life is beach-oriented and relaxed. Strong Pacific Islander and international student community. Less traditional US college town experience — more global, more tropical. Off-season travel to outer islands is a perk.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Hawaii is lifestyle 10/10 but functional for soccer and academic development. Extra flight cost to Australia is a real consideration. Best option if the Pacific island lifestyle is the priority and Olivier is comfortable with geographic isolation.
culture.lifestyleTags: Tropical island, Aloha culture, Pacific Rim community, beach lifestyle

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: UH head coach. Island program — unique Pacific culture and lifestyle environment.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Lifestyle 10/10 — Honolulu beach life. Geographically isolated for recruiting and travel. Extra flight cost to/from Australia. Lower academic/soccer tier.

name: CS Fullerton
acuAlignNote: Kinesiology covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Orange County clinical network for pre-PT placement.

fin.internationalNote: CSUF out-of-state COA is ~$28k/yr (2025-26) — one of the more affordable D1 options. CSU nonresident tuition is capped lower than the UC system. Athletic scholarship can cover a significant portion. Academic merit is limited for international students under CSU policy.

gpa.note: Very accessible CSU campus. Strong scholarship packages for international athletes.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for Big West Tournament.

coach.name: Head Coach
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: CSUF is a developing Big West program in Orange County — the most accessible minutes option in the Big West. Lowest-cost D1 program in Southern California at ~$28k/yr. Orange County location means warm weather, beaches (Huntington Beach 15 min), Disneyland (3 min), and CHOC (Children's Hospital of OC) clinical access.

facilityDetails.stadium: Titan Stadium Soccer Field — on-campus natural grass facility.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: CSUF athletic complex training fields. Warm OC climate allows year-round outdoor training.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: CSUF Athletics S&C facility — functional shared suite.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department labs. CSU-style practical curriculum — coursework emphasises applied learning.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: CSUF Athletics sports medicine team. CHOC (Children's Hospital of Orange County — top-10 USA paediatric hospital, 10 min drive). UCI Medical Center (20 min). Strong OC clinical network for pre-PT placements.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology labs: anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. Pre-PT pathway through OC clinical network. CSUF is the largest campus in the CSUF system — more resources than smaller CSU schools.
facilityDetails.extras: Fullerton in the heart of Orange County — Disneyland (3 min), Knott's Berry Farm (10 min), Huntington Beach (15 min), Newport Beach (20 min), Angels Stadium and Honda Center (10 min). LA Galaxy/LAFC (30-45 min). Cost of living is slightly elevated due to OC demand but still far below LA or SF.
facilityDetails.note: CSUF is the value play in the Big West — lowest cost, most accessible GPA, most realistic minutes, and an Orange County location that delivers on Olivier's warm/city lifestyle preference at the lowest possible price.

culture.vibe: CSUF is a large commuter-oriented CSU campus with a growing residential and international student community. Titan soccer culture is building. OC setting means the lifestyle is genuinely excellent — sun, beaches, theme parks, and LA accessibility — despite the program being at developing tier.
culture.thingsToDo: Disneyland (3 min), Knott's Berry Farm, Huntington Beach (15 min), Newport Beach, Angel Stadium (MLB Angels), Honda Center (NHL Ducks), Coachella Valley (1.5 hrs), San Diego (1 hr), LA (30-45 min).
culture.socialScene: Large diverse campus (40k). Primarily commuter culture — growing residential population. Brea and Placentia are quiet suburban neighbours. Social life gravitates towards beaches and OC attractions rather than traditional college town bars.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ CSUF is the lowest-cost warm-climate option in the guide with the most accessible GPA. Perfect if Olivier needs a guaranteed minutes platform with Southern California lifestyle. Soccer is the weakest element — truly developing tier.
culture.lifestyleTags: OC beaches, Disneyland proximity, lowest D1 cost, maximum minutes

staff.name: Head Coach
staff.bg: CSUF head coach. Developing Big West program. OC location and lowest D1 cost are key assets.

staff.name: Assistant Coach
staff.bg: Full-time assistant.

rec: Lowest-cost warm SoCal option. OC location is excellent lifestyle. Soccer is developing — most realistic minutes in Big West.
